Container.Name: fleetdm
Container.Overview:
Fleet DM - Open-source device management and endpoint security platform for macOS, iOS, Android, Windows, Linux, and ChromeOS.
IMPORTANT - DEPENDENCIES REQUIRED: Fleet requires MySQL and Redis to be running before it will start. Deploy the following containers first using Unraid Community Apps: 1. MySQL (set MYSQL_DATABASE=fleet, MYSQL_USER=fleet, and a strong MYSQL_PASSWORD) Fleet requires at least MySQL version 8.0.44, and is tested using the InnoDB storage engine with versions 8.0.44, 8.4.8, and 9.5.0. MySQL 9.6.0 is currently incompatible. 2. Redis
Once both are healthy, deploy this container and point the environment variables below at your MySQL and Redis container IPs/hostnames.
Before starting this container, run the following command from the Unraid terminal to pre-create the required directories with correct permissions: mkdir -p /mnt/user/appdata/fleetdm/{logs,data,vulndb} chown -R 100:101 /mnt/user/appdata/fleetdm/
TLS NOTE: If you are running Fleet behind Nginx Proxy Manager or another reverse proxy, set FLEET_SERVER_TLS to "false". If Fleet is handling TLS directly, set it to "true" and mount your cert and key files.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Fleet web UI and API. Devices (osquery agents) also connect here.

Config.Name: Localtime
Config.Description: Syncs the container clock with the Unraid host.

Config.Name: Fleet Data Volume
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Fleet application data.

Config.Name: Logs Volume
Config.Description: Persistent storage for osquery status and result logs.

Config.Name: Vulnerability DB Volume
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Fleet's vulnerability database.

Config.Name: TLS Certificate
Config.Description: Path to your TLS certificate file. Only required if FLEET_SERVER_TLS is set to true.

Config.Name: TLS Key
Config.Description: Path to your TLS private key file. Only required if FLEET_SERVER_TLS is set to true.

Config.Name: MySQL Address
Config.Description: Hostname and port of your MySQL container or server. If using a separate Unraid MySQL container on the same bridge network, use its container name (e.g. mysql:3306). Otherwise use the host IP.

Config.Name: MySQL Database
Config.Description: Name of the MySQL database Fleet should use. Must match the database created in your MySQL container.

Config.Name: MySQL Username
Config.Description: MySQL username Fleet will use to connect. Must match the user created in your MySQL container.

Config.Name: MySQL Password
Config.Description: Password for the Fleet MySQL user. Must match the password set in your MySQL container.

Config.Name: Redis Address
Config.Description: Hostname and port of your Redis container or server. If using a separate Unraid Redis container on the same bridge network, use its container name (e.g. redis:6379). Otherwise use the host IP.

Config.Name: Fleet Server Address
Config.Description: The address Fleet binds to inside the container. Leave as 0.0.0.0:1337 to listen on all interfaces.

Config.Name: Fleet Server Private Key
Config.Description: Required. A random base64 string used to encrypt session tokens. Generate one with: openssl rand -base64 32

Config.Name: Enable TLS
Config.Description: Set to 'true' if Fleet handles TLS directly (requires cert and key paths above). Set to 'false' if you are terminating TLS at a reverse proxy like Nginx Proxy Manager.

Config.Name: TLS Cert Path (Internal)
Config.Description: Path inside the container to the TLS certificate. Only used when FLEET_SERVER_TLS=true.

Config.Name: TLS Key Path (Internal)
Config.Description: Path inside the container to the TLS private key. Only used when FLEET_SERVER_TLS=true.

Config.Name: License Key
Config.Description: Optional Fleet Premium license key. Leave blank to run the free tier.

Config.Name: Session Duration
Config.Description: How long user sessions remain valid before requiring re-login (e.g. 24h, 8h).

Config.Name: JSON Logging
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to output structured JSON logs. Useful for log aggregation tools.

Config.Name: Osquery Status Log Plugin
Config.Description: Log plugin for osquery status logs. 'filesystem' writes logs to the Logs Volume path.

Config.Name: Osquery Status Log File
Config.Description: Internal container path for the osquery status log file.

Config.Name: Osquery Result Log File
Config.Description: Internal container path for the osquery result log file.

Config.Name: Osquery Label Update Interval
Config.Description: How often Fleet recalculates osquery label memberships (e.g. 1h, 30m).

Config.Name: Vulnerability Checks
Config.Description: Set to 'yes' to enable vulnerability scanning on this Fleet instance.

Config.Name: Vulnerability DB Path (Internal)
Config.Description: Internal container path where Fleet stores its vulnerability databases. Must match the Vulnerability DB Volume target.

Config.Name: Vulnerability Scan Periodicity
Config.Description: How often Fleet runs vulnerability scans (e.g. 1h, 6h).

Config.Name: S3 Bucket
Config.Description: Optional. S3 bucket name for storing software installers. Leave blank if not using S3.

Config.Name: S3 Access Key ID
Config.Description: Optional. S3 access key ID for software installer storage.

Config.Name: S3 Secret Access Key
Config.Description: Optional. S3 secret access key for software installer storage.

Config.Name: S3 Region
Config.Description: Optional. S3 region (e.g. us-east-1). For RustFS/LocalStack use any non-empty value such as 'localhost'.

Config.Name: S3 Endpoint URL
Config.Description: Optional. Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL (e.g. for RustFS or LocalStack). Leave blank for standard AWS S3.

Config.Name: S3 Force Path Style
Config.Description: Optional. Set to 'true' when using RustFS, LocalStack, or other S3-compatible backends that require path-style addressing.
